name: Get Enabled Alert Types
summary: Returns the list of alert types (e.g. ATP, LTP, Perc. Change) that are enabled for the logged-in user. POST call requiring jData (a JSON object whose only field is uid) and jKey as request parameters.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

notes: The documented full URL host is piconnect.flattrade.in (the curl example uses a 'BaseURL' placeholder which resolves to https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI). The request body is sent as form data: jData (a JSON object, the only field being uid) plus jKey, joined with '&'. The ai_ts response is an array of objects each containing an 'ai_t' field (the alert type code, e.g. ATP / LTP / Perc. Change); the response-fields table lists ai_ts as 'Array of alert types' but the actual element key is 'ai_t' as seen in the success sample. request_time is only present on success. On failure 'stat' is 'Not_Ok' and an 'emsg' field carries the error reason. The 'Possible value' column is blank for every request parameter, jData field, and response field in the source tables.
issues: None significant. The request-side jData/json tables on page 63 have no 'Possible value' entries (blank cells), confirmed via the page image. The response-fields table appears at the top of page 64 (continuation), then the FUNDS / Get Max Payout Amount section begins. The success sample's element key 'ai_t' is documented only implicitly in the sample, not in the response-fields table; captured in notes.
